content.problemStatement:
Arya the monkey is trapped inside an N×MN\times MN×M grid!

The grid has NNN rows (numbered 111 to NNN from top to bottom) and MMM columns (numbered 111 to MMM from left to right). The cell at the intersection of row iii and column jjj is denoted (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

Arya wants to escape the grid, of course - but first, he needs information.

Arya is initially at cell (X0,Y0)(X_0, Y_0)(X0​,Y0​) of the grid, and can move to other cells by jumping to them. Arya's jump strength is LLL, meaning Arya can jump **exactly** LLL units in any of the four directions up, down, left, and right.

Formally, from cell (a,b)(a, b)(a,b), Arya can jump to the following cells: (a−L,b),(a+L,b),(a,b−L),(a,b+L)(a-L, b), (a+L, b), (a, b-L), (a, b+L)(a−L,b),(a+L,b),(a,b−L),(a,b+L) (provided the destination is within the grid).

How many **distinct** cells in the grid can Arya reach using zero or more jumps of this form?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain five space-separated integers N,M,X0,Y0,N, M, X_0, Y_0,N,M,X0​,Y0​, and LLL — the dimensions of the grid, Arya's starting position, and Arya's jump strength.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the answer: the number of **distinct** cells Arya can reach within the grid.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There's only one cell in the grid.
Test case 222: The grid is of dimensions 3×43\times 43×4, and Arya starts at (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) with L=2L = 2L=2.
Arya can jump to (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1), and that's the only other cell that can be visited.
Test case 444: Since L=1L = 1L=1, Arya can reach every cell in the grid.
There are 109×109=101810^9 \times 10^9 = 10^{18}109×109=1018 cells in the grid - so this is the answer.
Note that the answer may exceed the limit of a 32-bit integer.
